title: Parris Island Protection Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.
plain_summary: The Parris Island Protection Act stops the government from spending money to close or move the Marine Corps training base at Parris Island, South Carolina. The bill also prevents the government from making any plans to close or move the base. Parris Island has been training Marines since 1891 and is very important to the Marine Corps. This law makes sure the historic training base will stay open and keep operating where it is.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Parris Island Protection Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these important facts. The Marine Corps training base at Parris Island, South Carolina has been training Marines since eighteen ninety one. Parris Island is an important place in Marine Corps history and represents the fighting spirit of America. The island has many different and difficult training conditions that have prepared Marines for every major war since World War One. Parris Island has built a record of excellent training and loyal service to America. Parris Island is the main training center for Marines from the eastern part of the United States and should stay that way. The government needs to keep investing money to make sure Parris Island can keep serving America for many years to come.

sections.title: Section 3  Prohibition of Closing or Realignment of Marine Corps Recruit Depot Located at Parris Island, South Carolina
sections.text: The government cannot use any federal money to close the Marine Corps training base at Parris Island, South Carolina. The government also cannot use federal money to move the base to a different location or to make plans for closing or moving the base.
